title: Intel's potent 18-core Core Ultra 5 250KF Plus CPU lands at retail for under $200
summary: Intel's 18-core Core Ultra 5 250KF Plus CPU offers high performance at a budget price, outperforming AMD in multi-threaded tasks. This chip is suitable for heavy workloads and gaming. Its value proposition makes it an attractive option for those seeking power on a budget.
insight: Intel's budget CPU beats AMD in multi-threaded performance

title: Level up your gaming setup with this Core Ultra 5 250K Plus bundle for $299 save $60 with ASRock's rock-solid B860 motherboard
summary: Newegg offers a Core Ultra 5 250K Plus bundle with ASRock's B860 motherboard for $299, saving $60
insight: Affordable high-performance gaming setup for budget-conscious enthusiasts

title: Report claims Arm chips will power 90% of AI servers based on custom processors in 2029 x86 and RISC-V on the outside looking in
summary: Hyperscalers are adopting Arm custom processors for AI servers due to efficiency and control. This shift is expected to lead to 90% of servers running custom silicon using the Arm ISA by 2029. The x86 and RISC-V architectures may be left behind.
insight: Arm's custom processor adoption gains dominance over traditional architectures.

title: Firm quietly boosts H.264 streaming license fees from $100,000 up to staggering $4.5 million backbone codec of the internet gets meteoric increase, AVC hikes follow disastrous H.265 licensing increases
summary: H.264/AVC streaming license fees have drastically increased from $100,000 to $4.5 million. The tiered system, managed by LA, is intended to restructure the licensing model. This change will significantly impact companies using the H.264 codec.
insight: H.264 licensing overhaul hits companies with massive financial burdens

title: Half of planned US data center builds have been delayed or canceled, growth limited by shortages of power infrastructure and parts from China the AI build-out flips the breakers
summary: Cloud giants face AI data center deployment hurdles due to power infrastructure shortages. The $650 billion planned AI infrastructure spend is being limited by shortages. Availability of components, particularly from China, is a significant obstacle.
insight: Power infrastructure shortages hinder AI data center deployment progress.

title: Researchers build Wi-Fi chip that can operate inside a nuclear reactor receiver uses special materials and design to withstand high doses of radiation for at least six months
summary: Researchers developed a Wi-Fi receiver that can operate in highly radioactive environments for at least six months, using special materials and design to withstand high doses of radiation.
insight: Radiation-resistant technology expands possibility of remote monitoring and control in extreme environments.

title: Save $240 on Intel's Core Ultra 7 270K Plus with 32GB of DDR5-6000 and Z890 motherboard get Intel's newest, fastest gaming CPU in a bundle that's 23% off
summary: Save $240 on Intel's Core Ultra 7 270K Plus bundle, featuring a Z890 motherboard and 32GB of DDR5-6000 memory.
insight: Upgrade gaming performance significantly with Intel's latest CPU bundle.

title: Anycubic Photon P1 Review: Dual Color/Dual Material, Tech Loaded
summary: The Anycubic Photon P1 is a dual color/dual material resin printer packed with cutting-edge tech, featuring a heated vat and Wave Release technology for enhanced printing capabilities.
insight: Revolutionary Wave Release tech simplifies resin print removal process.

title: Memory will consume 30% of hyperscaler AI data center spending this year, a 4X increase over 2023 Nvidia gets preferential supply terms well below standard market rates, says analyst firm
summary: SemiAnalysis predicts memory will account for 30% of hyperscaler capex in 2026, a 4X increase from 2023. This significant increase is attributed to the growing demand for AI data centers. Nvidia benefits from preferential supply terms.
insight: Growing AI demand drives memory costs to 30% of hyperscaler spending.

title: Here s what the FCC ban on foreign-manufactured routers actually means for consumers
summary: The FCC ban on foreign-manufactured routers requires U.S. certification for sale, imposing stricter regulations on router manufacturers. This means they must comply with U.S. standards and undergo rigorous testing before selling products in the country. The goal is to enhance cybersecurity and security of U.S. networks.
insight: U.S. consumers' sensitive data will be more protected now.

title: Microsoft says Copilot is for entertainment purposes only, not serious use firm pushing AI hard to consumers and businesses tells users not to rely on it for important advice
summary: Microsoft's Copilot terms of service contradict its marketing, raising concerns about AI reliability.
insight: Microsoft's Copilot raises questions about AI trustworthiness in critical applications.

title: Asus ZenWiFi BD5 Outdoor Wi-Fi 7 extender review: Great outdoor wireless performance, as long as you use an Ethernet backhaul
summary: The Asus ZenWiFi BD5 Outdoor delivers strong wireless performance when using an Ethernet backhaul. However, wireless backhaul can lead to inconsistent results. This outdoor Wi-Fi 7 extender excels with a wired connection.
insight: Wired backhaul is crucial for reliable outdoor Wi-Fi performance.

title: TSMC reportedly plans to build 12 fabs, four packaging facilities in Arizona plan purportedly part of Taiwan's agreed $500 million investment in the US
summary: TSMC reportedly plans to build 12 fabs and four packaging facilities in Arizona, as part of Taiwan's $500 million investment in the US. This expansion aims to strengthen Taiwan's semiconductor presence in the global market. The move is expected to create new job opportunities and boost local economic growth.
insight: Boosts US semiconductor capacity and strengthens Taiwan's global presence

title: Modder creates hybrid PlayStation, combining the guts of two original PS1 consoles into one custom PCB system features native microSD card & HDMI support
summary: A console modder has created a custom hybrid PS1 PCB, combining components from two original PS1 consoles. The custom PCB features native microSD card support and HDMI output, allowing for 1080p display on modern screens. This mod consumes less than 2 watts and runs at 3V.
insight: Reviving retro gaming with modern conveniences and energy efficiency.

title: Crypto platform Drift suffers from hack suspected to total $270 million firm goes into damage control mode, suspends deposits and withdrawals
summary: Drift Protocol is under attack, losing potentially $130-$270 million in assets.
insight: Large-scale crypto hack exposes vulnerabilities in decentralized finance systems.

title: Supermicro co-founder pleads not guilty to smuggling billions of dollars of Nvidia servers to China suspected smuggler released on $5 million bond
summary: Super Micro co-founder Wally Liaw pleaded not guilty to smuggling Nvidia servers to China.
insight: China's alleged tech smuggling has major national security implications.

title: Iran says it has struck Oracle data center in Dubai, Amazon data center in Bahrain country has threatened to attack Nvidia, Intel, and others, too
summary: Iran's IRGC claims to have struck an Oracle data center in Dubai, escalating tensions with US tech companies.
insight: Cyberattacks on critical infrastructure escalate global tech sector risk and conflict.

title: The largest programming community on Reddit just banned all content related to AI LLMs r/programming is prioritizing only high-quality discussions about AI
summary: The r/programming subreddit banned LLM-related content to promote high-quality AI discussions.
insight: Regulated AI discussions foster meaningful technical contributions online.

title: $1,299 Asus OLED gaming monitors are arriving with cracked panels for many buyers cut-down packaging with no protection for the panel gets the blame
summary: Asus' $1,299 OLED gaming monitors arrive with cracked panels due to inadequate packaging. Buyers have reported receiving their ROG Swift OLED PG32UCDM Gen3 monitors with damaged panels. The issue is attributed to poor packaging that fails to protect the panel.
insight: Inadequate packaging puts expensive gaming monitors at risk of damage.

title: Velxio is an open-source, self-hosted Arduino, Raspberry Pi, and ESP32 simulator
summary: Velxio is an open-source, self-hosted simulator for Arduino, ESP32, and Raspberry Pi boards that works in a web browser. It allows drag-and-drop boards, component connections, code writing, and serial console access without hardware. Velxio is inspired by Wokwi but can be self-hosted.
insight: Self-hosted Arduino simulation empowers developers with full control and data ownership

title: Realtek RTL8159 10GbE to USB 3.2 adapters sell for about $55 and up
summary: Realtek's RTL8159 10GbE to USB 3.2 adapters are now available, priced around $55 and up.
insight: Low-cost 10GbE adapters expand affordable networking options.

title: Artemis II update: Launch, TLI burn, toilet fix to Moon or bust
summary: Artemis II is underway, following a successful launch, TLI burn, and toilet fix. The spacecraft is expected to fly by the Moon's dark side on Monday. This mission marks a crucial step in NASA's lunar exploration plans.
insight: Pioneering human spaceflight to the Moon's previously unexplored dark side matters

title: MLB's robot-assisted strike zone is exposing umpire errors in real time
summary: The MLB's robot-assisted strike zone technology is reducing disputes and reshaping game-day strategy by providing real-time accurate strike zone evaluations and offering teams two challenges per game.
insight: Exposing umpire errors in real time promotes accountability and fairness.

title: Google's Gemma 4 AI can run on smartphones, no Internet required
summary: Google's Gemma 4 AI can run on smartphones without an internet connection, delivering advanced reasoning capabilities. The models require a powerful 80GB Nvidia H100 GPU to run in bfloat16 format. This enables students, researchers, and developers to access frontier intelligence on personal computers.
insight: Unlocking AI capabilities on personal devices revolutionizes workflows

title: Toshiba starts shipping SMR MAMR enterprise hard drives offering up to 34TB of storage
summary: Toshiba starts shipping high-capacity M12 Series SMR MAMR hard drives. These 3.5-inch drives offer storage capacities ranging from 30 to 34TB. They are designed for hyperscale customers and cloud services.
insight: Increased storage capacity meets growing data storage demands in the cloud.

title: Ford GT Mk IV sets N rburgring record for gas-powered cars
summary: The Ford GT Mk IV set a new lap record for gas-powered cars at the Nurburgring, making it the fastest American manufacturer to achieve this feat. It trails behind hybrid and electric record-holders, showcasing the car's exceptional performance in a predominantly electrified field. This accomplishment highlights the GT Mk IV's capabilities.
insight: Exceptional performance in a predominantly electrified automotive landscape.

title: Take-Two lays off AI team members weeks after "embracing" generative AI
summary: Take-Two lays off its AI team weeks after embracing generative AI.
insight: Company's AI ambitions may not align with its actual priorities.

title: NASA's Artemis laser system will send 4K video from the Moon at 260 Mbps
summary: NASA's Orion Artemis II Optical Communications system enables 4K video at 260 Mbps for transmission from the Moon to Earth.
insight: Improved video quality enhances scientific data and mission documentation capabilities.

title: Nvidia shows The Witcher 4 forest demo running path tracing on an RTX 4070
summary: Nvidia has released a GDC 2026 presentation showcasing The Witcher 4's PC version with path tracing features running on an RTX 4070. The demo displays a forest scene with detailed graphics and lighting. This presentation highlights Nvidia's upcoming tools and engine functionality.
insight: Nvidia's path tracing capabilities enhance game visuals with realistic lighting and effects.

title: X is using a Supreme Court ruling to try to kill a $250 million music copyright lawsuit
summary: X is using a Supreme Court ruling to challenge a $250 million music copyright lawsuit by arguing that the contributory infringement claim is no longer valid.
insight: Supreme Court ruling may wipe out major music copyright lawsuit theory.

title: Apple may be stockpiling mobile DRAM to outmaneuver rivals in a tight market
summary: Apple may outmaneuver rivals by absorbing higher memory costs, expanding market share.
insight: Strategic stockpiling helps Apple maintain market dominance in a volatile supply environment.

title: Chip foundry market hit $320 billion in 2025, driven by AI demand
summary: The chip foundry market reached $320 billion in 2025, driven by AI demand. The market has become increasingly complex, earning the term 'Foundry 2.0'. Silicon manufacturers have become major players in the industry.
insight: AI demand propels rapid growth in the chip foundry market.

title: Colorado's landmark right-to-repair law faces pushback from tech giants
summary: Colorado has approved measures making manufacturers provide repair information and parts. The state's right-to-repair legislation covers various products, including powered wheelchairs and consumer electronics. This aims to give consumers more control over repairs.
insight: Empowering consumers to fix their own products, reducing waste and costs.

title: Intel's next laptop chips could bring 28-core CPUs built for discrete GPUs
summary: Intel's Nova Lake-HX chips feature 28-core CPUs, wider I/O complex, and PCIe 5.0 connectivity for discrete GPUs, marking a higher-end branch of the mainstream Nova Lake-H line.
insight: Enhanced GPU support for high-performance computing and gaming

title: Nvidia RTX 5070 Ti Roundup: 9 Graphics Cards Tested
summary: Nvidia RTX 5070 Ti cards show consistent performance, but prices are a concern.
insight: High prices make Nvidia RTX 5070 Ti questionable value proposition.
